cases.name: single-item self-cycle executes with seed
cases.description: Inputs: 1x item0 and recipe item0->2x item0. Output target: 2x item0. Why: one seeded execution is craftable and doubles item0 from 1 to 2, so usage is [1] and the target is met.

cases.name: single-item self-cycle without seed errors
cases.description: Inputs: 0x item0 and recipe item0->2x item0. Output target: 1x item0. Why: the cycle cannot start without an initial seed item, so the solver cannot build a valid executable plan and returns error status.

cases.name: larger cycle without seed errors
cases.description: Inputs: no starting resources and a 4-recipe cycle 0->1->2->3->0 with amplification on return. Output target: 1x item0. Why: despite a positive cycle, no recipe is initially affordable, so execution is impossible and status is error.

cases.name: overlapping cycles without seed error
cases.description: Inputs: no starting resources and overlapping cycles sharing item1: (0<->1) and (1<->2). Output target: 1x item0. Why: every cycle still needs a seed to start, and none exists, so branch elimination cannot produce an executable plan.

cases.name: cycle that includes target item errors
cases.description: Inputs: no starting resources and 0->1->0 cycle where target item0 is itself in the loop. Output target: 1x item0. Why: including the target in a cycle does not avoid the seed requirement, so no executable plan exists and status is error.
